Title: Production of red-flowered oilseed rape via the ectopic expression of OvPAP2

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: OvP (purple-flowered wild type); also cloned from M4 cDNA as the expressed O. violaceus PAP2 homolog
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B. napus cv. Huashuang 3 (H3), Westar, J9709, G1300; Arabidopsis Columbia
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3 MYB; subgroup 6 anthocyanin regulator; contains R2R3 MYB domain, bHLH-interaction motif DLX2RX3LX6LX3R, and conserved KPXPR(S/T)F motif
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as a key MYB component of the MBW complex; interacts with bHLH partners in the complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in purple-petaled O. violaceus and in M4 petals; white-petaled OvW lacked evidence of this being the differential determinant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenics, ectopically expressed in petals and/or anthers depending on promoter; higher expression associated with red tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides red/purple floral pigmentation; discussed as increasing ornamental value and potentially reducing pollen beetle infestation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter context/tissue specificity (XY355 versus CaMV35S); chromosome dosage in M4 context
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR; ANS; F3H; F3'H/F3OH; TT19; MYB12
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Up-regulates several anthocyanin structural genes; critically induces DFR and ANS in petals only under petal-specific XY355 expression in B. napus; with 35S in B. napus, DFR and ANS induction occurred mainly in anthers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; causes red anthers in B. napus under 35S and red petals plus red anthers in B. napus under XY355; causes purple pigmentation in multiple Arabidopsis organs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics (RNA-seq); gene cloning and sequence alignment; phylogenetic analysis; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis and B. napus; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; metabolomic profiling of anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Comparative transcriptomics identified an O. violaceus PAP2 homolog expressed from the added chromosome in M4. 2. OvPAP2 encodes an R2R3 MYB anthocyanin regulator closely related to Arabidopsis PAP1/PAP2/MYB113/MYB114. 3. Constitutive overexpression induced anthocyanin accumulation broadly in Arabidopsis but mainly in anthers, not petals, in B. napus. 4. Petal-specific XY355-driven expression successfully induced red petals and anthers in B. napus. 5. Red petals accumulated specific cyanidin- and delphinidin-based anthocyanins and showed elevated DFR and ANS expression.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; tissue type and floral developmental stage influenced visible pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator decisive for red petal formation in B. napus; considered the major factor up-regulating anthocyanin pathway genes in M4 and transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: bHLH proteins of the MBW complex (type supported by motif/known function); GL3; EGL3; TT8; TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OvPAP2 sequence from M4 was identical to that from O. violaceus, supporting origin from the alien chromosome. In B. napus, promoter/tissue context strongly altered output: 35S failed to activate petal DFR/ANS sufficiently, whereas XY355 succeeded. Native promoter-driven OvPAP2 did not produce red coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering novel flower color in oilseed rape and potentially for pest management; petal-specific expression is preferable to constitutive expression for red petals; can be combined with marker-free transformation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: M4 addition line had smaller stature at bolting; possible pleiotropic pigmentation in non-target tissues under constitutive expression; pollinator effects considered likely limited
